question: The book of Esther takes place during the reign of what Persian king (Est 1:1)?
How many provinces did Xerxes rule over (Est 1:1)?
In the citadel of what city did Xerxes reign (Est 1:2)?
What did Xerxes do for 180 days (Est 1:4)?
options: He displayed the vast wealth of his kingdom
He imported horses and chariots for his military

question: How long was the banquet Xerxes gave in the garden in the citadel of Susa (Est 1:5)?
Who gave a banquet for the women in the palace of Xerxes (Est 1:9)?
What did Xerxes command on the seventh day of the banquet (Est 1:10)?
options: To bring Queen Vashti before him wearing her royal crown
To have the servant girls dance before him while he drank wine
To give all the guests gold and silver from the treasury
To have Queen Vashti dance before the guests of the palace

question: All of the following were listed as eunuchs who served Xerxes EXCEPT (Est 1:10)
Who was Xerxes’ queen that he ordered to present herself at his banquet (Est 1:11)?
All of the following were listed as Xerxes’ wise men EXCEPT (Est 1:14)
What role did Hegai play in the king’s palace (Est 2:3)?
options: He was an advisor to the king
He was the one over the forced labor

question: Who was a Jew in the citadel of the Susa (Est 2:5)?
What tribe was Mordecai from (Es 2:5)?
Who had carried Mordecai off into exile from Jerusalem (Est 2:6)?
What was Esther’s other name (Est 2:7)?
Why did Mordecai take care of Esther (Est 2:7)?
options: She had neither father or mother
She had been carried off into exile with him
She had been abandoned in Susa

question: Over what did Hegai have charge in Xerxes’ kingdom (Est 2:8)?
Besides beauty treatments what did Hegai provide for Esther (Est 2:9)?
How many maids did Hegai give Esther (Est 2:9)?
What treatments did the women receive for the first six months (Est 2:12)?
How did the girls go to the king (Est 2:13)?
options: They would be carried in on a couch
They could take anything they wanted with them

question: Who was Esther’s father (Est 2:15)?
When was Esther taken to the royal residence (Est 2:16)?
In what month was Esther taken to the royal residence (Est 2:16)?
What was the Persian tenth month called (Est 2:16)?
What did Mordecai find out about as he sat in the king’s gate (Est 2:21)?
options: That Esther was to be chosen as queen
That Heman was plotting to kill Esther
That there was about to be a famine in the land
That there was a plot to kill Xerxes

question: Who told the king of the plot to kill him (Est 2:22)?
What happened to the two men who plotted to kill King Xerxes (Est 2:23)?
options: They were dragged to death behind the king’s chariot
They were beheaded in the king’s presence

question: What was recorded in the annals of the king (Est 2:23)?
options: The plot against Xerxes that Mordecai had exposed
The record of all the virgins brought into the king’s palace
The family background of all the virgins in the king’s harem

question: Where did the royal officials knee before Haman (Est 3:2)?
Who asked Mordecai why he disobeyed the king’s command (Est 3:3)?
What enraged Haman (Est 3:5)?
options: That Mordecai would not move out of the way when Haman passed by
That Mordecai would not kneel down or pay him honor
That Mordecai was related to Queen Esther
That he was a Jew in a Persian court

question: What did Haman want to do (Est 3:6)?
options: Kill all of the Jews in the whole kingdom of Xerxes
Enslave all the Jews who would not bow before him

question: What was the first month called (Est 3:7)?
How did they determine what month the Jews would be destroyed (Est 3:7)?
options: They consulted with the wise men of Xerxes palace

question: What is the casting of lots called (Est 3:7)?
How did Haman described the Jews to the king (Est 3:8)?
options: Their customs are different and they do not obey the king’s laws
They do not pay tribute to the king or honor him
They do not pay homage to the gods of Persia
They do not allow their children to intermarry with the Persians

question: What decree did Haman request from King Xerxes (Est 3:9)?
options: To exile all the Jews out of the Persian empire
To drown all the Jews in the river
To burn all the homes of the Jews

question: Haman was from what tribal family (Est 3:10)?
Haman was the son of ________ (Est 3:10)
Who wrote out the script of the decree to kill the Jews to every province (Est 3:12)?
On what day and month was the edict against the Jews written out (Est 3:12)?
options: The thirteenth day of the first month
The tenth day of the seventh month
The first day of the fifth month
The last day of the twelfth month

question: How was the decree ordering the death of the Jews completed (Est 3:12)?
options: It was sealed with the king’s ring
Wax was melted sealing the edge of the decree
Haman marked each one with his own blood
An imprint of it was inscribed in stone

question: On what day and month were all the Jews to be destroyed (Est 3:13)?
options: The first day of the first month
The thirteenth day of the twelfth month
The tenth day of the tenth month
The seventh day of the seventh month

question: The twelfth month when the Jews were to be destroyed was called ______ (Est 3:13)?
Who was not allowed inside the king’s gate (Est 4:2)?
Where was one wearing sackcloth not able to enter (Est 4:2)?
Who told Esther of the decree to kill the Jews (Est 4:4)?
options: Hegai the head of the harem

question: What did Esther send Hathach to find out from Mordecai (Est 4:5)?
options: Why the king had given that order

question: Who was Hathach (Est 4:5)?
What did Mordecai send to Queen Esther by the hand of Hathach (Est 4:8)?
options: A copy of the Law of Moses

question: What did Mordecai urge Esther to do (Est 4:8)?
options: Flee from the palace before she was discovered
Enter the king’s presence and plead for her people
Remind the king how faithful Mordecai had been

question: What request did Esther send back to Mordecai (Est 4:15)?
options: That he and the Jews of the kingdom pray for God’s help
That he and the Jews of Susa fast for three days
That the Jews prepare to flee to the mountains
That he pray for her day and night

question: For how many days did Esther ask Mordecai and the Jews of Susa to fast (Est 4:15)?
What attitude did Esther have about going in to plead with the king (Est 4:16)?
options: The king will spare my people
How can I not stand up for my people

question: What did Esther wear to meet the king after the decree was given (Est 5:1)?
options: A necklace that Xerxes had given her

question: What did King Xerxes do to signal Esther could approach him (Est 5:2)?
options: He rose as she entered the king’s hall
He told his eunuch’s to bring her in
He held out his gold scepter
He motioned that she should take her place on the queen’s throne

question: Who did Haman observe as he left the first banquet with Esther and the king (Est 5:9)?
options: Hegai who was in charge of the harem
Queen Vashti who was in prison
Mordecai at the king’s gate who refused to rise

question: Who was Haman’s wife (Est 5:10)?
What did the king do when he couldn’t sleep (Est 6:1)?
options: He called for the head of his harem
He ordered Esther be brought to him
He had the chronicles read to him
He walled on the wall of the city

question: What had Bigthana and Teresh conspired to do (Est 6:2)?
options: Hand King Xerxes over to the Spartans
Kill the sons of Xerxes when he died

question: After reading the chronicles what question did the king ask (Est 6:3)?
options: What ever happened to Bigthana and Teresh?
Why was Mordecai not given a post in the palace of the king?

question: After reading the chronicles what question did Xerxes as Haman (Est 6:6)?
options: What should the king do for the man that saved his life?
What should be done with the traitors that plotted to assassinate the king?
Why had Haman plotted against the Jews?
What should be done for the man the king delights to honor?

question: Who was to take the “man the king delights to honor” through the streets (Est 6:9)?
options: The king’s friend and most trusted adviser
One of the king’s most noble princes

question: What was Esther’s petition on the second banquet day (Est 7:3)?
options: Destroy the destroyer of my people

question: What was Esther’s petition on the second banquet day (Est 7:3)?
options: Destroy the destroyer of my people

question: What did Harbona note in his defense of Mordecai against Haman (Est 7:9)?
options: Mordecai had spoken up to help the king
Mordecai had refused to bow before Haman
Haman had his men ready to kill all the Jews

question: What did Esther receive after Haman was hung or impaled (Est 8:1)?
options: A special throne next to the king
Freedom to return to her people

question: What did Esther tell the king after the death of Haman (Est 8:1)?
options: That she did not want Haman’s estate
That she wanted to return to Israel
That she was related to Mordecai

question: Who did Esther appoint over Haman’s estate (Est 8:2)?
options: Hegai who had shown Esther favor
Bigthana one of the king’s officials

question: After the death of Haman why did Esther fall at Xerxes’ feet weeping (Est 8:3)?
options: To spare Mordecai and put him over the officials at the king’s gate
To burn down the gallows that Haman had built
To put an end of the plan Haman devised against the Jews
To let her go free and return to Israel

question: What did Esther request from King Xerxes after Haman was killed (Est 8:5)?
options: An order overruling the former dispatches against the Jews
A burning of all the former king’s edicts for the killing of the Jews
A order that no Jew should be killed in any of the king’s provinces
An order that all those of the household of Haman should be slain

question: How many Persian provinces stretched from India to Cush (Est 8:9)?
The 127 provinces of Persia stretched from India all the way to ______ (Est 9:9)?
On what day could the Jews defend themselves and plunder their enemies (Est 8:12)?
options: The thirteenth day of the twelfth month
The ten day of the sixth month
The fifth day of the third month
The seventh day of the seventh month

question: What is the Persian twelfth month called (Est 8:12)?
What were the colors of Mordecai’s royal garments (Est 8:15)?
When the second edict was sent out how did the city of Susa respond (Est 8:15)?
What is the Persian twelfth month called (Est 9:1)?
Through Xerxes edict the Jews were able to gain the upper hand over _____ (Est 9:1)?
options: The residents of Susa who had plotted their demise

question: Why did the satraps and governors help the Jews (Est 9:3)?
Once the edict was enacted what did the Jews do (Est 9:5)?
options: They ran over their enemies with chariots
They burned the homes of their enemies
They struck down their enemies with the sword

question: How many sons did Haman have who were killed by the Jews (Est 9:10)?
What did Esther request be done with Haman’s sons (Est 9:13)?
options: Their bodies be thrown into an unmarked tomb
Their bodies be hung or impaled
Their bodies be buried with their father

question: What did the Jews not lay their hands on (Est 9:15)?
options: The plunder of those they killed
The wives and children of those they killed
The idols of those they killed
The food dedicated to their gods

question: How many were killed as a result of the edict in the provinces (Est 9:16)?
What did the Jews make the 14th day of the month of Adar (Est 9:17)?
options: A day of feasting and joy
A day of thanksgiving to God

question: On what day did the Jews in Susa finish and rest and feast (Est 9:18)?
What was to be done on the day of celebration as ordered by Mordecai (Est 9:22)?
options: Eat a special bread and wine on that day
Give presents of food to one another and gift to the poor
Walk around the walls of the city they inhabit to celebrate their freedom
Sound the trumpet and raise their glasses of wine

question: The feast of Purim was to remember _____ (Est 9:22)
options: The slaying of Haman and his sons
When God delivered his people once again
When the Jews got relief from their enemies

question: Why is the feast called the feast of Purim (Est 9:24)?
options: Because Pur highlights that “the Lord has provided”
Because Pur refers to Haman “casting the lot”
Because Purim means “sound the trumpet” of deliverance

question: Queen Esther was the daughter of _______ (Est 9:29)
Where was the account of the greatness of Mordecai recorded (Est 10:2)?
options: In the annals of the kings of Media and Persia
In the annals of the kings of Babylon
In the annals of the kings of Israel and Judah
In the book of Iddo the seer

question: Why was Mordecai held in esteem by the Jews (Est 10:3)?
options: Because he was recognized by King Xerxes
Because he told Esther about the plot to kill the Jews
Because he worked for the good of his people
Because he destroyed Haman the enemy of the Jews

question: Why was Mordecai held in esteem by the Jews (Est 10:3)?
options: Because he was recognized by King Xerxes
Because he told Esther about the plot to kill the Jews
Because he destroyed Haman the enemy of the Jews
Because he spoke up for the welfare of all the Jews
